mnemonics: V3: Volume, Variety, Velocity. Traditional BI handles low V3, Big Data handles high V3.
S.O.S.: Traditional BI is Structured, One-source, Slow. Big Data is the opposite.
keyPoints: Traditional BI primarily analyzes structured data from internal systems (e.g., CRM, ERP). Big Data analytics handles unstructured and multi-structured data (e.g., social media, logs, sensors) from diverse sources.

Traditional BI uses a predefined schema (schema-on-write) and is optimized for complex SQL queries on smaller datasets. Big Data often uses a flexible schema (schema-on-read) and is optimized for processing massive volumes of data.
Traditional BI reporting is often retrospective and batch-processed. Big Data analytics enables real-time or near-real-time processing for predictive and prescriptive insights.
commonMistakes: Assuming Big Data is just a larger version of Traditional BI; it represents a paradigm shift in technology and approach.
Believing Traditional BI is obsolete; both are often used together in a modern data architecture, with BI for structured reporting and Big Data for exploring unstructured data.
